metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py` to the consumer enumeration (the loop at line 480 asserts all MOVE_REGISTRY moves are always-applicable and will fail when the scoped foundation/enhancement move is added); either update that assertion or note the intentional exemption.
findings.finding: test_review_kernel.py:480 iterates passes.MOVE_REGISTRY and asserts every move passes `move_applies(move, active_triggers=[])` (i.e., every move is always-applicable). Adding the foundation/enhancement move with an `applies_when` scoping field will break this assertion, yet this consumer is not listed in the plan's enumeration of consumers to keep green.
findings.location: What section — 'Consumers to keep green' / tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py:479-481
findings.suggested_fix: Add `tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py` to the 'Consumers to keep green' list, and scope the existing assertion (e.g., filter to moves without `applies_when`) or add a separate assertion for the new scoped move.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the concrete move id (and add it to the registry if it does not yet exist) that post-hoc-validation findings should route to, so AC4's test has a deterministic symbol to assert against.
findings.finding: AC4 requires a test asserting that post-hoc-validation coaching routes to a 'follow-up-ticket move id', but neither a follow-up-ticket move nor its id is introduced anywhere in the What section — the plan describes only two changes (add foundation/enhancement move; sharpen move 9).
findings.location: AC line 4 — 'Proof: a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected, not a defer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the pre-existing move id (or confirm it exists) that AC4's coach-mapping test should assert — or, if it does not pre-exist, add a described 'open a follow-up ticket' move to the plan body and reconcile with the DEFERRED_MEASUREMENT prohibition.
findings.finding: AC4 requires a test that asserts 'the follow-up-ticket move id is selected,' but no move with that role is ever named or described in the plan body, and the 'What' section explicitly prohibits adding DEFERRED_MEASUREMENT. If the move does not pre-exist in the registry, AC4 is untestable without adding undescribed work — a cross-section contradiction between the 'DO NOT add' constraint and AC4's implicit requirement.
findings.location: AC line 4 vs. What section ('DO NOT add DEFERRED_MEASUREMENT')
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add: 'AC4 targets move id X (pre-existing) which routes to a discovered_from follow-up; no new move is added.' If no such move exists yet, scope a minimal 'suggest_followup_ticket' move and explicitly distinguish it from DEFERRED_MEASUREMENT.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an implementation step in the What section (or a note in AC4) explicitly identifying WHICH existing or new move id handles post-hoc validation coaching and WHETHER any code change is required to route it — so the AC4 proof tests a real behavioral claim, not pre-existing state.
findings.finding: AC4 specifies an observable proof ('a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected') but no corresponding implementation step in the What section describes what code change causes post-hoc validation coaching to route to a discovered_from follow-up ticket move. Without an implementation step, the proof either tests pre-existing behavior (tautological/self-authored oracle) or tests a behavior that is silently assumed to already exist — neither of which closes the intent loop for this goal.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 / What section
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a line such as: 'Confirm (or add) that the existing [move-id] move is the canonical handler for post-hoc validation; no new move entry is added — AC4 proof verifies the existing routing is stable and not overridden by the new foundation/enhancement move.' If a new move IS needed, describe it explicitly alongside the other two changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC4: Specify which move id (existing or newly added) represents 'route to a discovered_from follow-up ticket' so the coach-mapping test has a concrete, stable target — either call out an existing move that covers this case or explicitly add a named follow-up-ticket move to the 'What' section.
findings.finding: AC4 refers to 'the follow-up-ticket move id' as if a specific, named move already exists or is being added by this story for routing post-hoc validation to a discovered_from follow-up ticket — but no such move appears in the current MOVE_REGISTRY (ids 1–9, 11, 12), and the new move this story adds is 'foundation/enhancement', not a follow-up-ticket routing move. The test target is unspecified, which risks either (a) writing the test against a wrong existing move id, or (b) prompting an undocumented second new move to be added solely to satisfy the test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: 'Proof: a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected, not a defer.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the concrete applies_when tag value(s) for the new foundation/enhancement move — e.g. the specific criteria IDs or a new tag string — since plan-review has no pre-existing applies_when vocabulary to inherit.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the new foundation/enhancement move's `applies_when` will use 'the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry', but verification shows plan-review has NO established applies_when tag vocabulary: all current moves declare no `applies_when` (always-applicable), and active_triggers in plan-review are plain criteria IDs from surviving findings (e.g. 'F1', 'E4') — not a curated tag set. There is no 'existing move-gating tag set' to inherit from for plan-review.
findings.location: What section — 'the applies_when vocabulary is the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry'
findings.suggested_fix: State the exact applies_when list, e.g. `"applies_when": ["F1"]` (scope to F1/right_sized findings) or `"applies_when": ["S1", "S2"]` (scope to sizing criteria), referencing the criteria IDs that map to coverage/sizing findings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'follow-up-ticket' move in the What section: assign it an id, name, and locked template so AC4's test has a concrete move id to assert against.
findings.finding: AC4 references 'the follow-up-ticket move id' as if it already exists or is unambiguously defined, but no such move exists anywhere in MOVE_REGISTRY (passes.py:686-731) or any other registry file. The plan's What section says 'DO NOT add DEFERRED_MEASUREMENT' but never names, assigns an ID to, or defines a template for the 'follow-up ticket' move AC4's test must assert against.
findings.location: AC4 — 'a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC4's reference to 'the follow-up-ticket move id' with the What section: either name the concrete move id that serves the follow-up-ticket routing role (e.g., a new move id added to MOVE_REGISTRY), or restate AC4 in terms of the foundation/enhancement move id actually being added.
findings.finding: AC4 requires 'a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected' but no move with a 'follow-up-ticket' role is ever added to MOVE_REGISTRY in the What section. The What section adds only a 'foundation/enhancement' move; there is no 'follow-up-ticket' move id defined anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: AC4 / What section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `applies_when` tag string(s) for the foundation/enhancement move (e.g., criterion IDs like `'P8'` or `'right_sized'` that plan-review's pass4_coach derives from surviving findings' criteria), since plan-review has no pre-existing applies_when tag vocabulary to reference.
findings.finding: The plan states the new foundation/enhancement move's `applies_when` will use 'the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry', but plan-review's existing MOVE_REGISTRY has zero moves with `applies_when` fields — there is no pre-existing tag vocabulary for plan-review moves. The only established tag vocabulary is in code-review (OVERLAY_IDS). The specific tag strings to use are therefore undefined.
findings.location: What section — 'it CARRIES an applies_when field scoping it to sizing/coverage findings (matching how existing moves are offered)'
findings.suggested_fix: Plan-review's active_triggers are criterion IDs drawn from surviving findings (passes.py:777: `triggers = {c for f in surviving for c in f.get('criteria', []) or []}`). Specify e.g. `applies_when: ["P8", "right_sized"]` or whatever criterion keys correspond to sizing/coverage findings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact applies_when tag value(s) for the foundation/enhancement move, explicitly resolving whether tags are criterion IDs (matching the active_triggers vocabulary) or a new semantic string set — and if the latter, explain how they will intersect active_triggers at runtime.
findings.finding: The applies_when tag vocabulary for the new foundation/enhancement move is unresolved: active_triggers in pass4_coach are criterion IDs (e.g. 'G5', 'F1'), but the plan says 'the applies_when vocabulary is the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry' — yet ALL existing plan-review MOVE_REGISTRY entries have no applies_when, so there is no pre-existing tag vocabulary to reference. If the implementer uses semantic strings like 'sizing' or 'coverage', they will never intersect active_triggers (criterion IDs), making the move permanently unreachable despite being registered.
findings.location: What section — 'it CARRIES an applies_when field scoping it to sizing/coverage findings (matching how existing moves are offered)'
findings.suggested_fix: Since active_triggers in pass4_coach are criterion IDs, state concrete criterion IDs as the applies_when values (e.g. applies_when: ["G5", "E3"]) to match the sizing/coverage criteria the move is intended to fire for. Alternatively, if a semantic-tag vocabulary is desired, document how those tags will be injected into active_triggers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify in the What section which move ID (existing or new) handles post-hoc-validation-to-discovered_from routing and whether any code change is required, so AC4's proof tests a concrete behavioral claim rather than pre-existing or undefined state.
findings.finding: AC4 requires a test asserting 'the follow-up-ticket move id is selected' for post-hoc validation coaching, but no implementation step in the What section describes which move (new or existing) produces this routing or what code change is needed. The What section adds only two changes (foundation/enhancement move + sharpen move 9 in-place), and DEFERRED_MEASUREMENT is explicitly prohibited. Without a named move ID and a described change, AC4's proof either tests pre-existing behavior (tautology) or requires undescribed work.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 / What section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the exact `applies_when` criterion ID(s) (e.g. `'G5'`, `'F1'`) that will gate the foundation/enhancement move — verify each ID exists in criteria_routing.json and that `pass4_coach` will carry it in `active_triggers` when the relevant finding type survives.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the new foundation/enhancement move's `applies_when` will scope to 'sizing/coverage findings' using 'the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry', but in plan_review `active_triggers` are criterion IDs drawn from `f.get('criteria', [])` on surviving findings (e.g. 'F1', 'G5', 'T5a'). No criterion named 'sizing' or 'coverage' exists in criteria_routing.json, so the claimed tag vocabulary does not resolve to any verifiable criterion ID string the filter will actually fire on.
findings.location: What section — 'Add foundation/enhancement move with a LOCKED template … applies_when field scoping it to sizing/coverage findings (matching how existing moves are offered); the applies_when vocabulary is the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry'
findings.suggested_fix: Audit which criterion ID fires for over-scoped / under-covered plans (likely 'G5' for scope coherence or 'F1' for AC quality); specify `applies_when: ['G5']` (or whichever resolved ID) rather than a concept label, and add an AC confirming the tag intersects a surviving finding's `criteria` list in the test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact string values for the new move's `applies_when` list (e.g. criterion IDs like `["F1", "right_sized"]` or checklist keys) so the applicability filter behaves as intended.
findings.finding: The applies_when tag vocabulary for the new foundation/enhancement move is stated as prose ('sizing/coverage findings') but the runtime mechanism (applicable_moves in coach.py:109-113) matches applies_when tags against the criterion-ID strings carried by surviving findings (e.g. 'F1', 'right_sized'). No concrete tag list is provided in the plan.
findings.location: What section: 'it CARRIES an applies_when field scoping it to sizing/coverage findings (matching how existing moves are offered); the applies_when vocabulary is the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add the 'follow-up ticket / discovered_from' move explicitly to the MOVE_REGISTRY additions in this plan (with an ID and locked template) or clarify that AC4 tests an existing move by its known ID.
findings.finding: AC4 requires 'a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected' but no follow-up-ticket (or discovered_from) move exists in MOVE_REGISTRY and the plan assigns it no ID. The test cannot be written without the move existing and having a known key.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria line 4: 'a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected, not a defer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `applies_when` tag value(s) (e.g. the string literal(s) from load_move_registry's tag set) to use when registering the foundation/enhancement move, rather than leaving tag selection to the executor.
findings.finding: The specific `applies_when` tag value(s) to scope the new foundation/enhancement move are not stated; the plan says only 'matching how existing moves are offered' and defers to the executor to inspect load_move_registry and pick appropriate tags.
findings.location: What section — 'it CARRIES an applies_when field scoping it to sizing/coverage findings (matching how existing moves are offered)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'applies_when: ["sizing", "coverage"]  # these are the existing tag literals in load_move_registry' to the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Provide the complete replacement template string for move 9 so the executor has a verbatim target to implement and the diff check can be meaningful.
findings.finding: The sharpened move 9 template string is not provided; the plan states the intent ('also cover restating an unobservable/deferred success target as an in-session-observable one') but leaves the executor to author the new wording.
findings.location: What section — 'Sharpen move 9 IN-PLACE … to also cover restating an unobservable/deferred success target as an in-session-observable one'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Provide the exact replacement template string for move 9 in the plan (it must contain `{subject}`) so AC2 diff-check and the back-out instruction are unambiguous.
findings.finding: Move 9's sharpened template is described by intent only ('also cover restating an unobservable/deferred success target as an in-session-observable one') but the actual updated template string is never provided, violating the locked-template principle the plan itself asserts.
findings.location: What section: 'Sharpen move 9 IN-PLACE … from "Plan how {subject} will be verified before implementing it." to also cover restating an unobservable/deferred success target as an in-session-observable one.'
findings.suggested_fix: Example: `"Plan how {subject} will be verified before implementing it — and if the current success target is only observable post-session, restate it as an in-session-observable outcome."`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign an explicit registry key (e.g. `"10"`) to the new foundation/enhancement move in the plan so the MOVE_REGISTRY entry and AC1 test assertion are unambiguous.
findings.finding: The new foundation/enhancement move is added to MOVE_REGISTRY without an assigned registry key (ID). MOVE_REGISTRY is a dict keyed by string integers ('1'–'12', skipping '10'); the plan never names what key the new entry should occupy.
findings.location: What section / AC line 1: 'foundation/enhancement move added to MOVE_REGISTRY in passes.py:686'
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'move id: "10"' (or another chosen key) to the What section and AC1; e.g. `"10": {"name": "foundation/enhancement", "template": "Ship a working baseline of {subject} now; make the enhanced version a dependent follow-on.", "applies_when": [...]}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add an AC line that verifies the F1/in_session_evaluable blocking relationship (e.g., a unit test asserting block_threshold applies only at the F1 level), or explicitly remove the claim that this note is '(folded into AC)' and mark it as non-testable guidance.
findings.finding: The F1 phrasing note is described as '(folded into AC)' but no AC line actually verifies the behavioral constraint it introduces — that the in_session_evaluable sub-item must not independently block. This leaves described guidance without a covering criterion.
findings.location: What section, 'F1 phrasing note (folded into AC)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC1's proof command: either broaden it to `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review.py` or add `-k 'move_registry or pass4_coach'` to also cover the rendering/determinism test.
findings.finding: AC1's proof specifier `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review.py -k move_registry` will only select `test_load_move_registry_merges_project_extensions` (which contains 'move_registry' in its name). The rendering/determinism test that actually exercises the new move's template is named `test_pass4_coach_maps_findings_and_renders_deterministically` and does not match the `-k move_registry` filter — so the stated proof marker cannot confirm the new move renders correctly.
findings.location: AC1 — 'Proof: pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review.py -k move_registry green'
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC1 proof to `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review.py -k 'move_registry or pass4_coach'` so both the registry merge test and the template-rendering test are exercised.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
